title: Accumulating
why.untouched: script.js is exactly as it was.
hint2.untouched: Five days at three shifts at twenty cups. The console shows sixty.
why.not-linked: The script never reaches the page.
why.crashed: Your code stopped with an error.
hint2.crashed: Look at the Console panel below.
why.forgot-wednesday: Wednesday is never checked at all.
hint2.forgot-wednesday: Three days, two checks. The third is forgotten.
hint2.still-repeating: Put the three days in a list and walk it with a loop. Then the check is written once.
hint3.still-repeating: Square brackets make a list: [monday, tuesday, wednesday]. It has a length and is indexed like a string.
hint2.wrong-cups: Five days at sixty cups a day.
hint3.wrong-cups: The equals sign replaces. Every turn wipes the previous one, and only the last survives.
hint4.wrong-cups: It has to accumulate, not replace.
hint2.wrong-income: The inner loop works out one day correctly. The outer one replaces it instead of adding it.
hint2.wrong-best: Tuesday is eighty. If you get something else, a day is being skipped.
why.wrong-count: There are not three lines in the console.
why.lost-p: A paragraph has disappeared from the page.
blocks.0.text: Three sums. Total cups for the week, total income, and the strongest day.
blocks.1.text: The first two show the numbers for ONE day, not five. The third skips Wednesday.
blocks.3.text: The equals sign puts a new value in. Every turn wipes out what was accumulated and starts again.
blocks.4.text: Only the last turn survives. The number looks plausible — it is simply for one day, not a week.
blocks.5.quote: A counter outside the loop starts at zero and grows inside. It is never assigned.
blocks.7.text: The second loop is nested: for each day, all the shifts are walked. The outer one turns five times, the inner one three times per outer turn — fifteen in total.
blocks.8.text: The inner loop has its own counter, which starts fresh on every turn of the outer one. That is why dayIncome is reset at the top — it is the total for ONE day.
blocks.10.text: The third sum checks the days one at a time. Add a fourth day and you have to write another condition. Forget it, and the result quietly excludes that day.
blocks.12.text: Square brackets gather values into a list. It has a length and is indexed like a string, so the same loop walks it.
blocks.14.text: Fix the two accumulations and rewrite the search for the best day using a list and a loop.
blocks.15.text: Lists come in detail in section six. Here you only need that they are made with square brackets and walked like a string.
